avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: As the freezing level drops the snow cover will start to refreeze, particularly around the higher tops. New  accumulations will develop in wind sheltered locations, mainly on North-East to South-East aspects above 900 metres. These new deposits are expected to be shallow and generally inconsequential. Cornice and icefall collapse will initially remain a hazard, stability improving as the freezing level drops.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Becoming cooler with the freezing level dropping, expected to be around 900 metres most of the day, dropping to 800 metres by end of the afternoon. Light rain overnight, turning to snow as the freezing level drops. Strong South-Westerlies easing and veering to become light North-Westerlies by late afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: A cloudy day with light rain throughout the morning before easing and some bright spells in the afternoon. Very strong winds throughout from the South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to diminish in the well established thaw conditions. The snow cover is soft but remains generally well bonded. Greatest snow amounts are found mainly on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres. Cornice and icefall collapse remain a significant hazard. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh unstable windslab will develop overnight mainly on North to East aspects above 1000 metres with better stability at lower elevations. Fragile cornices will develop above these aspects. The older snowpack will freeze in the cooler conditions and have good stability. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Overnight precipitation will fall as snow above 1000 metres initially but down to 700 metres by dawn with a mainly dry day to follow. Gale force South-Westerly winds will ease by dawn and veer to the North-West. The freezing level will drop slowly through the period to around 700 metres in the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale Force South-Westerly winds today. Snow on the higher summits with rain at lower elevations. The freezing level dropped to around 1100 metres this morning before rising to just above the summits in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab are present mainly on North to East aspects above 1150 metres with features such as gully tops and corrie rims most affected. Fragile cornices were developing mainly above these aspects. Isolated wet snow instabilities are also persisting on steep slopes mainly on the same aspects. At lower elevations the snowpack is thawing and stability is good. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The thaw has depleted the snow cover and presented a soft but generally stable snowpack throughout. Greatest amounts are mostly above 950m on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Patchy and snow ribbons below. As the day progresses the snowpack will begin to refreeze  and become more consolidated. Ice and rockfall hazard still possible below crags.  The avalanche hazard will be Low

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Winds very strong South-Westerly overnight veering to become North-Westerly and lessening in strength during the day becoming light by the end of the afternoon. Becoming cooler, freezing level above the tops overnight Thursday lowering to 700m or so by the end of the forecast period. Precipitation falling as rain  at first but slowly turning to sleet and snow showers by daybreak Friday, then it is expected to be dry thereafter.
Observed Weather Influences: A wet and mild day with rain to summit levels with the occasional sleety shower. Winds strong South-Westerly. Poor visibility.
ObservedSnowStability: A diminishing snowpack with greatest amounts above 950 metres on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Ribbons and patches of snow below this level but above 800m. The snowpack although soft and moist to depth at all altitudes, is generally stable throughout.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will be well bonded and stable. The snow surface will become firm as the freezing level falls through the day. Any new snow deposits are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Becoming drier, colder and less windy as a ridge of high pressure develops. Overnight showers will be rain at most levels and gradually ease to give a mainly dry day with occasional showers which will turn to snow above around 500 metres as the freezing level falls. Strong South-Westerly winds overnight will decrease to become moderate Westerly during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Mild, wet and windy. Rain at all levels with very strong South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw and diminish. Snowpack stability is good. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Cooler and drier conditions through the day will give the snowpack a chance to dry out a bit and reduce the heavy thaw conditions affecting the area over the last few days. With light snow showers isolated pockets of windslab may form mainly above 850 metres in wind sheltered areas with gully exits and corrie rims with North through East to South-Easterly aspect most affected, these areas are not expected to be deep or extensive and should be easily avoidable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Rain will continue overnight with a trace of new snow on the higher tops. It will become drier by morning with the possibility of light snow showers above 800 metres. Winds will be South-Westerly fresh to strong overnight veering and decreasing to moderate North-Westerly through the course of the day. Becoming colder, the freezing level through the day will be around 800 metres. New snow amounts expected to be less than 1 cm.
Observed Weather Influences: Mild conditions with rain and sleet have affected the area. Winds have been fresh to strong generally South-Westerly. The freezing level was just above the higher summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw and diminish particularly at lower levels. The remaining snow is soft wet and is generally stable on all but the steepest slopes where surface wet snow instabilities exist, most affected areas are sheltered gullies and North-Westerly through North to Easterly slopes mainly above 950 metres.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised accumulations of unstable windslab will develop on North to Easterly aspects above 900 metres. Areas most affected will be around corrie rims and gully tops. Wet snow instabilities will persist on steep North-West to North-East aspects above 950 metres; however, as it turns cooler from early morning onwards stability will gradually improve. Unstable cornices will overhang some of these aspects. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: As it turns cooler during the night some snowfall is expected above 800 to 900 metres, this will clear to leave a dry day.  Gale force South-West winds will gradually ease during the day and veer to become fresh North-Westerly by the afternoon. The freezing level will gradually fall from summit levels to 800 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force South-South-East winds with light precipitation falling as snow above 1100 metres.  It was freezing at around summit levels.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw at all but the highest altitudes. Instabilities persist on steep North-West to North-East aspects above 950 metres. Large unstable cornices overhang some of these aspects. Avalanche activity and cornice collapse were observed on North-Westerly facing slopes above 1000 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack is generally stable. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
